summary: The implementation correctly satisfies all 7 acceptance criteria from task 6: the install script is executable (mode 100755 per git diff), idempotent (uses cat > overwrite semantics), installs an executable hook, uses the exact gitleaks command specified, and soft-fails when gitleaks is absent. Two minor issues were identified: the soft-failure path in the hook does not match the task spec hint exactly (uses >/dev/null 2>&1 instead of &>/dev/null — a POSIX vs bash portability trade-off since the hook shebang is #!/bin/sh), and there is no guard ensuring the script is run from the monorepo root when the working directory is not the repo root.

suggestions.suggestion: The hook uses #!/bin/sh but the gitleaks availability check uses >/dev/null 2>&1, which is correct POSIX sh. However the task spec hint shows &>/dev/null. Using >/dev/null 2>&1 is the right choice here — it is correct for #!/bin/sh. No change needed, but it is worth noting this was a deliberate and correct deviation from the hint.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents breakage in strict POSIX shells (dash, Alpine sh) that do not support the bash-only &> redirect operator.

suggestions.suggestion: The install script has no guard to detect if it is being run outside the monorepo root. If a developer runs it from a subdirectory, git rev-parse --show-toplevel still resolves correctly, so this is not a crash risk. However, consider adding a comment noting that the script must be run from within the git repo, matching the task's edge-case note: 'Script should be run from the monorepo root (where .git/ lives); add a guard or note if needed.' A one-line comment above REPO_ROOT= suffices.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces confusion for developers who run the script from outside the git working tree (e.g. a detached volume), where git rev-parse would fail with a non-obvious error. set -euo pipefail already causes an exit on that failure, so the only missing piece is a clear error message.

ac1_script_exists_and_executable.notes: Git diff shows mode 100755, confirming the executable bit is set and tracked by git.

ac2_running_script_creates_hook.notes: Smoke verification confirms .git/hooks/pre-commit is created on first run.

ac3_hook_is_executable.notes: chmod +x $HOOK_PATH is called on line 18 of install-hooks.sh. Smoke verification confirms test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit exits 0.

ac4_hook_uses_correct_gitleaks_command.notes: Line 15 of the generated hook: gitleaks detect --staged --verbose --no-banner — exact match to spec.

ac5_soft_failure_when_gitleaks_missing.notes: Lines 8-13 of the hook check command -v gitleaks and exit 0 with a warning. Smoke verification explicitly confirmed soft failure behavior.

ac6_idempotent.notes: cat > overwrites the hook file unconditionally on every run. Smoke verification confirmed second run produces the same result without error.

ac7_smoke_check.notes: Smoke verification result confirms: bash scripts/install-hooks.sh && test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit exits 0.
